---
title: "draft1"
format: html
---

```{r}
# R packages
library(tidyverse)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

# Data
library(ipumsr)
ddi <- read_ipums_ddi("data/ahtus_00001.xml")
df <- read_ipums_micro(ddi)
```

### Data Cleaning

```{r}
filtered_data <- df %>%
  filter(
    CIVSTAT == 1,
    HHTYPE == 1,
    UNDER18 >= 1,
    AGE >= 22 & AGE <= 40,
    EDUC %in% c(5, 6),
    AGEYNGST >= 0 & AGEYNGST <= 18,
    SEX %in% c(1, 2)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    sex_label = ifelse(SEX == 1, "Male", "Female"),
    ACT_UNDOM_HRS = ACT_UNDOM / 60
  )
```

### Initial Visualization 1

```{r}
# Paid, Unpaid, and Childcare Work by Sex (2018)

summary_data <- filtered_data %>%
  mutate(
    sex_label = ifelse(SEX == 1, "Male", "Female"),
    # Convert minutes to hours
    childcare = ACT_CHCARE / 60,
    unpaid    = ACT_UNDOM / 60,
    paid      = ACT_WORK / 60
  ) %>%

# Reshape
pivot_longer(
  cols = c(childcare, unpaid, paid),
  names_to = "work_type",
  values_to = "hours"
) %>%

# Group for averages
group_by(sex_label, work_type) %>%
summarise(
  hours = mean(hours, na.rm = TRUE),
  .groups = "drop"
)

# Set stacking order for the bars, first level is the bottom of the bar
# Desired order: childcare (bottom), unpaid (middle), paid (top)
summary_data$work_type <- factor(
  summary_data$work_type,
  levels = c("childcare", "unpaid", "paid")  # <-- CHANGE ORDER HERE
)

ggplot(summary_data, aes(x = sex_label, y = hours, fill = work_type)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  labs(
    title = "Paid, Unpaid, and Childcare Work by Sex (2018)",
    x = "Sex",
    y = "Average Hours per Day",
    fill = "Work Type"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "childcare" = "red",     # bottom
      "unpaid" = "orange",       # middle
      "paid" = "gray"          # top
    ),
    labels = c(
      "childcare" = "Childcare",
      "unpaid" = "Unpaid Domestic Work",
      "paid" = "Paid Work"
    )
  )
```

### Initial Visualization 2

```{r}
# Unpaid Domestic Work by Sex (1930-2018)

# Average hours by year and sex
summary_data <- filtered_data %>%
  group_by(YEAR, sex_label) %>%
  summarise(avg_domestic_hours = mean(ACT_UNDOM_HRS, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

# Line plot
ggplot(summary_data, aes(x = YEAR, y = avg_domestic_hours, color = sex_label)) +
  geom_line(size = 1) +
  geom_point() +
  labs(
    title = "Unpaid Domestic Work by Sex (1930 - 2018)",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Average Hours per Day",
    color = "Sex"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Male" = "steelblue", "Female" = "salmon"))

# Note for 2018
# 2.72 hrs for female
# 1.57 hrs for male
```

```{r}
#| eval: false

# Print all column names
sapply(df, function(x) attr(x, "label"))
```

## Clustering

```{r}
# Clustering Analysis

# All time variables
time_vars <- df[, c(
  "ACT_CHCARE", "ACT_CIVIC", "ACT_EDUCA", "ACT_INHOME",
  "ACT_MEDIA", "ACT_OUTHOME", "ACT_PCARE", "ACT_PHYSICAL",
  "ACT_TRAVEL", "ACT_UNDOM", "ACT_WORK"
)]

# Clean data
row_totals <- rowSums(time_vars)
time_vars <- time_vars[row_totals > 0, ]
  
# Convert time variables to time porportion within a day (24 hours)
row_totals <- rowSums(time_vars)
time_prop <- sweep(time_vars, 1, row_totals, "/")

# Remove 0s
time_prop[time_prop <= 0] <- 1e-6

# CLR Transform
clr_transform <- function(x) {
  log(x) - mean(log(x))
}

time_clr <- t(apply(time_prop, 1, clr_transform))

# All must be false...
any(is.na(time_clr))
any(is.infinite(time_clr))
any(is.nan(time_clr))

# Run PCA
pca <- prcomp(time_clr, center = TRUE, scale. = TRUE)
summary(pca)

# Visualize
plot(pca$x[,1], pca$x[,2],
     xlab = "PC1", ylab = "PC2",
     main = "PCA of Time Use")

# Understand components
# Each "principal component" is a linear combination of the time variables
pca$rotation

# Select the top 3 components
pca_data <- pca$x[, 1:3]  # first 3 PCs

set.seed(123)
kmeans_result <- kmeans(pca_data, centers = 3)

# Visualize clusters
plot(pca_data[,1], pca_data[,2],
     col = kmeans_result$cluster,
     pch = 16,
     xlab = "PC1", ylab = "PC2",
     main = "Behavioral Clusters")

# Labels
df_clusters <- time_vars
df_clusters$cluster <- kmeans_result$cluster
```
